name: Tabs
summary: Tabbed interface with `Tabs.Tab` triggers and `Tabs.Panel` content and full `tablist` ARIA; controlled via `value`/`onValueChange` — use it to switch between sibling views in the same region.

bestPractices: Use Tabs to switch peer views that don’t require navigation changes; use routes for deep navigation.
Keep labels short and stable; avoid wrapping to multiple rows—enable horizontal scroll when needed.
Provide sensible initial selection and preserve it across renders/routing.
Respect overflow: allow swipe/drag to scroll when the strip exceeds width; show edge fades to hint at more content.
Ensure keyboard navigation: Arrow keys move focus, Enter/Space activates.
Prefer tokenized spacing and motion for a cohesive feel.
Avoid mixing tabs and inline navigation within the same strip.
Use appropriate variants and placement to reflect app hierarchy.

Announce active tab for screen readers: the strip exposes `role=tablist` with `aria-orientation` tracking the `orientation` prop, each tab carries `aria-selected` and `aria-controls`, and tab/panel ids are namespaced per instance so multiple Tabs on a page never collide.

Prefer the canonical `onValueChange`/`onValueCommit` events: each fires with the full `ChangeInfo` payload (`previousValue`, `phase`, and an honest `info.source`) — a tab click reports `'pointer'` and Arrow-key navigation reports `'keyboard'`.
